page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. This KVM-hypervised instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 1 thread per core. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the ecs.ic5.2xlarge exhibits mixed performance characteristics. In Passmark CPU testing, including extended instructions and encryption tests, the instance achieves average performance within the middle 50% tier. Similarly, uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average category. However, the instance shows limitations in raw computational throughput, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and single-threaded gzip compression registering in the weak to poor categories.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but constrained 8.0 GB memory capacity, this instance is best suited for compute-focused workloads with low memory footprints. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, lightweight text processing, and small-scale LLM inference tasks, where it achieves average performance for prompt processing and text generation with a 135M model. It is less suitable for memory-intensive database operations or large-scale data processing due to its weak memory benchmark scores and low resource density.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.2xlarge is an x86_64 server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of memory and no local storage. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average results in Passmark CPU tests, encryption, and large-block memory bandwidth. However, it exhibits weak performance in stress-ng CPU tests, Geekbench, and database operations. With a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per core, this instance represents a compute-focused hardware profile with constrained memory resources. It is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, and small-scale language model inference, while being less optimal for memory-heavy databases or intensive data processing workloads.
